obligation_text: The provider shall, following the reporting of a serious incident, without undue delay, perform the necessary investigations in relation to the serious incident and the AI system concerned. This shall include a risk assessment of the incident, and corrective action.
scoring_text: Skill in AI serious incident investigation, including root cause analysis, risk assessment of incidents, AI system investigation methodology, and corrective action design and implementation following serious incidents.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in AI serious incident investigation, including root cause analysis, risk assessment of incidents, AI system investigation methodology, and corrective action design and implementation following serious incidents.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identification, bias evaluation, human oversight documentation, and residual risk determination.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 73(4) (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.695, cosine: 0.702). Secondary: RC-S-008 (RC, cosine: 0.609), AG-K-004 (AG, cosine: 0.605).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.0/10 (raw 0.695). Shared vocabulary: [ai, risk, skill]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [action, analysis, assessment, cause, corrective, design] vs WIDAI terms [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.892 (P=0.887, R=0.897). Balanced P/R — the 27-word competency implication and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (86.1%). Semantic tension between "Skill in AI serious incident investigation, including root cause analysis, risk" and "Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identifi". Despite STS-based 'Equal' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.702 ≈ STS 0.695 (gap 0.006). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, risk, skill] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.1%. Shared tokens: [ai, risk, skill]. EU AI Act-only: [action, analysis, assessment, cause, corrective, design]. WIDAI-only: [assessments, bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 86.1% on Equal classification. The matched KSA operates in a different professional domain than the obligation/competency requires. STS 0.695 passed the threshold but secondary signals contradict the match.
